# Provider Capability

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/providercapability -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

> Riido task: RIID-4687

Executable SSOT: `provider/capability` package plus this manifest.

Generated reader for the C3 Provider Capability domain: runtime capability snapshot model, protocol vocabulary, compatibility summary values, fingerprint input shape, and protocol-critical args.

- Evidence artifact: `provider-capability-evidence`
- ProviderCapability fields: `44`; fingerprint input fields: `11`
- Protocol kinds: `6`; critical arg sets: `5`

## Vocabulary

- Event stream formats: `unknown`, `text-only`, `ndjson`, `json-rpc-notifications`
- Protocol maturities: `unknown`, `stable`, `experimental`, `deprecated`
- Compatibility statuses: `supported`, `degraded`, `experimental`, `blocked`

## Protocol Critical Args

| ProtocolKind | Args |
| --- | --- |
| `claude-stream-json` | `-p`, `--print`, `--output-format`, `--input-format`, `--permission-mode`, `--mcp-config`, `--strict-mcp-config`, `--verbose` |
| `codex-exec-jsonl` | `--json` |
| `codex-app-server` | `--listen` |
| `claude-compatible-wrapper` | none |
| `openclaw-agent-json` | `--local`, `--json`, `--session-id`, `--message`, `--model`, `--system-prompt` |
| `cursor-agent-stream-json` | `-p`, `--output-format`, `--yolo` |

## Invariant Anchors

- `ProtocolKind` is the primary adapter-selection key; `ProviderKind` alone is insufficient.
- `DetectedVersion` is raw signal only; runtime branching uses protocol and surface facts.
- `CompatibilityStatus` is a lossy summary; gate decisions must also read maturity, opt-in, missing capability, blocked, and degraded reason fields.
- `ExposesUnsafePermissionBypass` is a risk signal, not permission to enable bypass execution.
- Event stream facts are provider-neutral: `SupportsStructuredEventStream` plus `EventStreamFormat`.
- `CapabilityFingerprint` excludes `NativeConfigVersion`; native config is task execution context, not runtime capability.
- Unknown provider surfaces are preserved instead of being dropped.
- Protocol-critical CLI args are owned by `provider/capability.ProtocolCriticalArgs`.

## Evidence Loop

- Observe: Provider capability had an executable package and tests, but the public reader was a long hand-maintained SSOT.
- Hypothesis: Generating the reader from the package catalog and reflective model counts will keep protocol/fingerprint governance aligned with code.
- Execute: Load provider/capability catalogs, verify expected counts, render the reader, and upload provider-capability evidence in CI.
- Evaluate: The verifier fails on stale markdown, malformed manifest, workflow gaps, package shape drift, or protocol-critical arg catalog drift.
- Retrospective: Provider capability knowledge becomes executable while the rich details stay in package APIs and tests.
